response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic using low-latency async I/O), current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and a long track record of craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). However, the must-have of strong Python is unsupported — the skills list and work history center on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang with no Python mentioned — and evidence of Staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, and operational/on-call ownership at scale is thin. Worth a conversation given the AI-native exposure and systems depth, but with clear gaps to probe.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Current AI-native experience: agentic workflow tooling and MCP code generators at re:cinq
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, teaching/workshop track record
concerns: No Python evidence anywhere in the resume, a stated must-have
Little demonstrated technical-strategy leadership or cross-functional stakeholder coordination at Staff scope
No explicit operational ownership, on-call, or incident-response experience mentioned
